# statuspal_custom_domain_validation (Resource)

Waiter resource that blocks until a status page's custom domain reaches the "active" state. Use this after creating DNS records to ensure the domain is verified before proceeding. Destroying this resource is a no-op — it only removes the waiter from Terraform state.

## Example Usage

```terraform
# Waiter resource that polls until a status page's custom domain reaches
# "active" status. Use this as the final step in a custom domain setup to
# confirm the domain is fully configured before proceeding.

resource "statuspal_custom_domain_validation" "example" {
  organization_id       = "1"
  status_page_subdomain = "example-subdomain"
  timeout_seconds       = 600
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `organization_id` (String) The organization ID that owns the status page.
- `status_page_subdomain` (String) The subdomain of the status page whose custom domain should be validated.

### Optional

- `timeout_seconds` (Number) Maximum seconds to wait for the domain to become active. Defaults to 1800 (30 minutes).

### Read-Only

- `id` (String) Placeholder identifier attribute.
